title: pyranthus ambatoanus
intro: Pyranthus ambatoanus, also known as 'Golden Feverfew', is a flowering plant species in the daisy family native to Central and South America. It is a herb used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and as an anti-inflammatory agent. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe digestive issues, calm anxiety, and help in managing menstrual cramps, due to its antispasmodic and analgesic properties. Pyranthus ambatoanus can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, often used to alleviate various health complaints in a natural and holistic way.
